publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 128-131. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Weddy. Morg.
It is a great relief to me that you approve of all I have done, for I was very anxious about it.-- I did not expect much that you could come up before Thursday.--
I think Anne should come certainly, as I have requested Green to take our housekeeping at Ashley, making of tea, coffee &c, & she cannot do this unless she has a nursery assistant.
Besides this, I think Anne with the help of the cook, should act as housemaid.--
But perhaps it is on account of the small-pox, that you question the propriety of her going. If so, you had better talk to Green about it.--
Whatever you decide about the riding horses, I shall think best. I do not know if I quite like the idea of those steep stony hills on a horse only used to plain roads.--
I hope you will not think I have done wrong in engaging

a pair of horses for today.-- As I was to pay a guinea at any rate, I determined to make the most of the money, & mean to go to Fordhook, see the De Morgans & get the things I wanted from there. The drive will be a nice wholesome one, & besides this I shall be able to make use of the beasts for Town today.--
Are you aware that Mrs Cattermole set out for Ockham at 3 o'clock in the morning of yesterday. I am glad of it, for she will be the most comfortable nurse for her

husband. Some one kindly lent her a gig, & I heard on its return yesterday that the coachman was much better & doing most favorably. This is exactly what Dr Locock expected to hear, & I think it seems probable that the man caught the disease from John, but that the vaccination delayed its appearance. You know he says he has been unwell the last 3 weeks or more.-- Do ask Mrs Cattermole about John, for I have heard through her that he is quite out of danger, & eating enormously. Mrs Cattermole has been sending things to him, which are now
requisite, & not furnished at the hospital. Had you made any arrangements about this? If not, we ought to do so, for after such a desperate illness he will no doubt require excellent feeding.--

My vaccinated arm does not trouble me in the least at present. I believe before tomorrow it is hardly likely to show much, but there is a little red circle round each of the two places, which I think indicates it was done effectually.-- The child was a little merry healthy boy of 5 months old, who kept laughing & rocking his body backwards
& forwards, so that Dr L had great difficulty in taking the matter from him.-- I am extremely comfortable in my little suite of rooms.

Will you say I wish my old parasol, the one lined with white silk, to be brought. Also a small parcel containing bottles, which was taken to Ockham by the coachman who went down on Monday, & which is of some consequence to me, as it contains things for my teeth.-- Likewise please tell Green to bring me
a night-shift for tomorrow night with her, & indeed she had best likewise bring a day-shift & a pari of trowsers for Friday, in case the carrier should not come in time.--
Take a stock of letter-wax pray, both for here & Ashley.
Do have your 6 shirts from the school, enquired for & brought, if they have not already come, & let them have the marking undone, for that can be added at any time.--
I shall answer the enclosed,
after you come.

I have got three orders for the Zoological Gardens, & the regulations & list of members. If I am to be a member, I must be proposed now or soon, to be elected for next year. It is done by ballot, 3 members proposing the candidate, & many ladies are members. I shall I think ask Mr Philip Coxe to manage it for me, if I decide to be a member, he is one. It is only necessary for one of the proposing members to be acquainted with the candidate.
All this I got at the office in Leicester Sqre today, & my 3 orders, gratis. I want to take B.
